title: HTTP Status Codes
description: Status codes returned by HTTP servers, as defined in RFC 9110 and related specifications.

codes.name: Continue
codes.description: The server has received the request headers. The client should proceed to send the request body.

codes.name: Switching Protocols
codes.description: The server is switching protocols as requested by the client, for example upgrading from HTTP/1.1 to WebSocket.

codes.name: Processing
codes.description: The server has received and is processing the request, but no response is available yet.

codes.name: Early Hints
codes.description: Used to return some response headers before final HTTP message, allowing the client to begin preloading resources.

codes.name: OK
codes.description: The request succeeded. The meaning of success depends on the HTTP method used.

codes.name: Created
codes.description: The request succeeded and a new resource was created. The new resource is returned in the response body or via the Location header.

codes.name: Accepted
codes.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not been completed and may not be acted upon.

codes.name: Non-Authoritative Information
codes.description: The request succeeded, but the enclosed payload has been modified by a transforming proxy from the origin server's 200 response.

codes.name: No Content
codes.description: The request succeeded and there is no additional content to send in the response body.

codes.name: Reset Content
codes.description: The request succeeded. The client should reset the document view that caused this request to be sent.

codes.name: Partial Content
codes.description: The server is delivering only part of the resource due to a Range header sent by the client.

codes.name: Multi-Status
codes.description: Conveys information about multiple resources, for situations where multiple status codes might be appropriate.

codes.name: Already Reported
codes.description: The members of a DAV binding have already been enumerated in a preceding part of the response and are not included again.

codes.name: IM Used
codes.description: The server fulfilled a GET request and the response is a representation of the result of one or more instance manipulations applied to the current instance.

codes.name: Multiple Choices
codes.description: The request has more than one possible response. The client should choose one of them.

codes.name: Moved Permanently
codes.description: The URL of the requested resource has been changed permanently. The new URL is given in the response.

codes.name: Found
codes.description: The URI of the requested resource has been changed temporarily. The client should continue to use the original URI.

codes.name: See Other
codes.description: The server is redirecting the client to a different resource, indicated by a URI in the Location header, using a GET request.

codes.name: Not Modified
codes.description: Indicates the resource has not been modified since the version specified in the request headers. The client can use its cached version.

codes.name: Temporary Redirect
codes.description: The requested resource is temporarily located at a different URI. The client must use the same HTTP method for the redirected request.

codes.name: Permanent Redirect
codes.description: The resource is permanently located at another URI, given in the Location header. The client must use the same HTTP method for future requests.

codes.name: Bad Request
codes.description: The server cannot process the request due to a client error, such as malformed request syntax or invalid request framing.

codes.name: Unauthorized
codes.description: The request lacks valid authentication credentials. The client must authenticate itself to get the requested response.

codes.name: Payment Required
codes.description: Reserved for future use. Originally intended for digital payment schemes; now occasionally used by APIs to indicate quota exhaustion.

codes.name: Forbidden
codes.description: The client does not have permission to access the requested resource. Unlike 401, authentication will not change the outcome.

codes.name: Not Found
codes.description: The server cannot find the requested resource. The URL may be wrong, or the resource may not exist.

codes.name: Method Not Allowed
codes.description: The HTTP method used is not supported for the requested resource.

codes.name: Not Acceptable
codes.description: The server cannot produce a response matching the criteria specified in the request's Accept headers.

codes.name: Proxy Authentication Required
codes.description: The client must authenticate itself with the proxy before this request can be served.

codes.name: Request Timeout
codes.description: The server timed out waiting for the request. The client may repeat the request without modifications.

codes.name: Conflict
codes.description: The request conflicts with the current state of the server, such as a version conflict on a resource update.

codes.name: Gone
codes.description: The requested resource is permanently gone and will not be available again. Unlike 404, this condition is expected to be permanent.

codes.name: Length Required
codes.description: The server refuses to accept the request without a defined Content-Length header.

codes.name: Precondition Failed
codes.description: The server does not meet one or more preconditions specified in the request headers.

codes.name: Content Too Large
codes.description: The request body exceeds the limits defined by the server.

codes.name: URI Too Long
codes.description: The URI requested by the client is longer than the server is willing to interpret.

codes.name: Unsupported Media Type
codes.description: The media format of the requested data is not supported by the server.

codes.name: Range Not Satisfiable
codes.description: The range specified by the Range header in the request cannot be fulfilled; it may be outside the size of the target resource.

codes.name: Expectation Failed
codes.description: The expectation indicated by the Expect request header cannot be met by the server.

codes.name: I'm a Teapot
codes.description: The server refuses to brew coffee because it is, permanently, a teapot. An April Fools' joke from RFC 2324 that became an informal standard.

codes.name: Misdirected Request
codes.description: The request was directed at a server that is not able to produce a response for the requested URI.

codes.name: Unprocessable Content
codes.description: The request was well-formed but could not be followed due to semantic errors in the contained instructions.

codes.name: Locked
codes.description: The resource being accessed is locked.

codes.name: Failed Dependency
codes.description: The request failed because it depended on another request that failed.

codes.name: Too Early
codes.description: The server is unwilling to risk processing a request that might be replayed.

codes.name: Upgrade Required
codes.description: The client should switch to a different protocol, specified in the Upgrade header.

codes.name: Precondition Required
codes.description: The origin server requires the request to be conditional, to prevent lost-update problems.

codes.name: Too Many Requests
codes.description: The client has sent too many requests in a given amount of time (rate limiting).

codes.name: Request Header Fields Too Large
codes.description: The server is unwilling to process the request because its header fields are too large.

codes.name: Unavailable For Legal Reasons
codes.description: The server is denying access to the resource as a consequence of a legal demand. The code is a reference to Fahrenheit 451.

codes.name: Internal Server Error
codes.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

codes.name: Not Implemented
codes.description: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfil the request.

codes.name: Bad Gateway
codes.description: The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, received an invalid response from an upstream server.

codes.name: Service Unavailable
codes.description: The server is not ready to handle the request, typically due to temporary overload or scheduled maintenance.

codes.name: Gateway Timeout
codes.description: The server, while acting as a gateway or proxy, did not receive a timely response from an upstream server.

codes.name: HTTP Version Not Supported
codes.description: The HTTP version used in the request is not supported by the server.

codes.name: Variant Also Negotiates
codes.description: The server has an internal configuration error: the chosen variant resource is configured to engage in transparent content negotiation itself.

codes.name: Insufficient Storage
codes.description: The server is unable to store the representation needed to complete the request.

codes.name: Loop Detected
codes.description: The server detected an infinite loop while processing the request.

codes.name: Not Extended
codes.description: Further extensions to the request are required for the server to fulfil it.

codes.name: Network Authentication Required
codes.description: The client needs to authenticate to gain network access, typically used by captive portals.
